raw:
{
  "judge_id": "Judge-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.4": 3,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 1,
    "RX.4": 2,
    "RX.5": 2
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies the load-bearing concept (physics-informed neural network), shows where the draft drifts from it (by explaining that the method described is not a PINN in the Raissi et al. sense but a coordinate-based neural field fit to simulation data), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast (suggesting renaming or justifying the 'spectral' label explicitly).",
    "R1.4": "The agent names one prior work (DeepONet) and articulates the delta (in terms of accuracy and inference speed).",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the 'spectral' label), but does not mark confidence and missing evidence for all recommendations.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers a substantially different active set than requested, as the method described is not a PINN in the Raissi et al. sense.",
    "RX.4": "The output introduces new errors (misrepresenting the method as a PINN) and fabrications (claiming 'Spectral' in the name is earned), but with one minor regression (the sinusoidal activation is an architectural inductive bias for smoothness/oscillation, not a physics prior in the PINN sense).",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.4": 0.8,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 0.5,
    "RX.4": 0.6,
    "RX.5": 0.6
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies the load-bearing concept (physics-informed neural network), shows where the draft drifts from it (by explaining that the method described is not a PINN in the Raissi et al. sense but a coordinate-based neural field fit to simulation data), and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast (suggesting renaming or justifying the 'spectral' label explicitly).
rationale.R1.4: The agent names one prior work (DeepONet) and articulates the delta (in terms of accuracy and inference speed).
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the 'spectral' label), but does not mark confidence and missing evidence for all recommendations.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers a substantially different active set than requested, as the method described is not a PINN in the Raissi et al. sense.
rationale.RX.4: The output introduces new errors (misrepresenting the method as a PINN) and fabrications (claiming 'Spectral' in the name is earned), but with one minor regression (the sinusoidal activation is an architectural inductive bias for smoothness/oscillation, not a physics prior in the PINN sense).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason.
